item.title: UK’s financial ambulance-chasers are running out of road
item.description: The chances of claims management companies landing another big blow against the banks are fading

item.title: The luxury industry’s many contradictions
item.description: Exclusivity is not just a matter of selling some items that almost no one can afford

item.title: A note from Trump and Xi’s couples therapist
item.description: They’re not listening to each other and growing apart

item.title: Could the Roman coin I’ve bought have been stolen?
item.description: I recently purchased an ancient coin for £5,000 in an online auction

item.title: The battle for the soul of Labour
item.description: Challenge to Starmer is a perilous moment for the UK and its standing

item.title: Brown-Forman’s $17bn bid rejection leaves a bitter taste
item.description: The Brown family may have balked at handing its brands over to Sazerac, but it would have been more appealing than the alternatives

item.title: Why UK assets are attractive despite the storm
item.description: The bearish outlook may only be shortlived, presenting opportunities for longer-term investors

item.title: The indelible appeal of tattoos
item.description: Getting inked is more popular than ever but a rapid expansion in providers has reduced work for London’s parlours

item.title: Badenoch is underpriced to become prime minister
item.description: The Conservative leader’s great friend is time — she has three years to outflank Farage and beat a failing Labour Party

item.title: Why scrapping quarterly earnings is a bad idea
item.description: US chief executives should be wary of putting their own convenience ahead of transparency

item.title: GameStop makes big bet that governance doesn’t matter
item.description: With his offer rebuffed by eBay, the ball is now in GameStop boss Ryan Cohen’s court

item.title: Labour’s wishful thinking problem
item.description: Accepting a world of economic trade-offs is crucial for the country’s future

item.title: Disinflation disappears
item.description: Plus, UK gilts

item.title: Tech Exchange
item.description: This series features conversations between FT journalists and the technology industry’s leaders, innovators, and thinkers. In the latest instalment, Amazon’s Panos Panay, the head of the group’s device unit talks about the hardware push that follows Alexa+

item.title: BuzzFeed and GoPro learn nostalgia does not pay the bills
item.description: The goodwill of middle-aged consumers will not be enough to generate a meaningful return

item.title: Why global imbalances matter
item.description: They lie at the intersection of almost everything that matters in geoeconomics and geopolitics

item.title: Trump is up against China’s great wall of confidence
item.description: The US president will encounter a very different country to the one he visited in 2017

item.title: Don’t let bad thinking scupper your house sale
item.description: Price fixation locks in vendors when housing market falters

item.title: Nintendo needs Super Mario once again
item.description: The little plumber is a bulwark of intellectual property as rising chip prices pressure the company

item.title: Data: you don’t know what you’ve got till it’s gone
item.description: To decide whether official statistics are useful, look at what happens when they disappear

item.title: Private credit isn’t the problem — concentration is
item.description: Diversification is the foundation of good investing

item.title: A reluctant Intertek risks testing investors’ patience
item.description: FTSE 100 company has rejected previous EQT takeover offers saying they undervalued it

item.title: The Iran war dilemma for central bankers
item.description: What happened after the pandemic is an imperfect guide for the latest shock

item.title: How architecture can save lives
item.description: Carefully designed homes in Tanzania have been shown to lower disease rates among children

item.title: Why Americans dread AI
item.description: Silicon Valley encourages the view that the technology is unstoppable — and Trump seems to agree
